cases.name: T3RMINAL POS
cases.comment: Benchmarks the T3RMINAL POS dapp (https://github.com/paritytech/t3rminal contracts/). Two production contracts (BulletinIndex, TransactionLog) plus a benchmark-side helper T3rminalDriver that generates per-iter unique saleId/date/CID strings on-chain. Models 5 days at a busy shop. One dedicated terminal per day handles all that day's 100 sales + 10 refunds + 1 day-close; mempool nonce ordering serializes within-day phases without explicit awaits. Across days, 5 distinct senders submit concurrently. Hot path: TransactionLog.recordTransaction's 14-field struct write with 5 dynamic strings + saleIdToDay mapping + dailyBuffer.push. Day close stresses delete of a 110-entry storage array.

inputs.comment: Allocate OWNER. Constructor admin on both BulletinIndex and TransactionLog; only OWNER can addShopAdmin.
Deploy TransactionLog wired to BulletinIndex. OWNER becomes constructor admin + first shopAdmin.

Deploy T3rminalDriver — benchmark-side helper that builds per-iter unique saleId/date/CID strings on chain. Wraps recordTransaction / recordRefund / finalizeDay so the workload can drive them from repeat blocks via numeric variables only.
OWNER grants Driver the shopAdmin role. Driver.closeDay needs this because it calls TransactionLog.finalizeDay, which is onlyShopAdmin and uses msg.sender = Driver.

Allocate 5 terminal accounts (one per day) and register a label for each. consolidate_state propagates TERMINAL_0..N-1 to the case scope so the measured days loop below can use caller=TERMINAL_D for everything in day D. Per-day single caller means all of a day's sales, refunds, and closeDay get strictly-increasing nonces from one sender — the mempool serializes them in submission order, so closeDay's gas estimate against 'pending' sees the prior txs and the entryCount check matches without needing await_transaction_inclusion. start_watcher: true: the warm-up iter is the watcher cutoff; everything after lands tracked.

Days outer: 5 days. D=0 warm-up; D=1..4 are parallel outer iters, each running their inner repeats from TERMINAL_D. Across days: N_DAYS distinct senders submitting concurrently. Within a day: serial nonce queue ⇒ no race between sales/refunds and the day-close.

steps.comment: Sales inner: 100 parallel iters per day. All submitted by TERMINAL_D (same caller as refund and closeDay below) so the mempool orders sales by nonce within the day. saleNonce = D*1000 + K — unique across all (D, K).

Refunds inner: 10 parallel iters per day. Each refunds the R-th sale of the same day (originalSaleNonce = D*1000 + R, which was recorded by the sales loop above). Same caller (TERMINAL_D) as sales → nonces strictly follow sales in the mempool, so the refund's gas estimate sees the target saleId in saleIdToDay.

Day close (caller = TERMINAL_D, same as sales and refunds). Driver publishes the day's CID to BulletinIndex then finalizeDay verifies CID match and dailyBuffer.length == 110. Because TERMINAL_D is the same sender as all prior sales/refunds of this day, closeDay's nonce is strictly greater — the mempool guarantees the chain processes all earlier txs first, so the entryCount check matches. On success it deletes the entire 110-entry dailyBuffer.
